source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Unintentional discharge of synthetic based mud
actions_taken: Derrickman closed the dump line valve
BSEE inspectors conducted onsite inspection on November 18, 2020
Nabors held a stand down with all crew members
Personnel changes implemented for those responsible
JSA on fluid transfers updated to include tank cleaning procedures
event.summary: On November 16, 2020, approximately 458 barrels of synthetic based mud (247 barrels of base oil) were unintentionally discharged into the Gulf of Mexico during fluid transfer operations on the Nabors MODS 200 rig at Ewing Banks Block 873. The discharge resulted from improper valve alignment, specifically an overboard discharge line (dump line valve) left in the open position. Contributing factors included failure of personnel to follow procedures and verify valve alignment prior to pumping, and falsification of documentation indicating verification had been completed when it had not.
recommendations: Updated JSA on fluid transfers to include tank cleaning procedures

hazards.name: Pressurized fluid system with multiple interconnected tanks and valves

threats.name: Valve left in open position during fluid transfer
Failure to verify valve alignment before fluid transfer
Inadequate communication of valve status between day and night crews

consequences.name: Discharge of synthetic based mud into Gulf of Mexico

controls.name: Fluid Transfer Permit
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure proper planning and authorization of fluid transfer operations

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analysis (JSA) and Fluid Transfer Permit was completed in preparation to transfer synthetic based mud (SBM) from an offshore supply vessel to the rig's mud pits. The documents were signed by the Toolpusher, Derrickman, and Shaker Hand.

controls.name: Valve alignment verification procedure
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring verification of valve line-up before fluid transfer
controls.lod_basis: Procedural requirement to verify correct valve configuration

supporting_text: Furthermore, there was a valve alignment procedure for the fluid transfer, but the Derrickhand and Shaker hand falsified the documentation by indicating that both individuals verified the valve alignment but in reality neither had done so.
Personnel failed to follow procedures and verify valve alignment prior to pumping SBM.

controls.name: Pre-tour safety meeting
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to brief crew on planned operations and safety requirements
controls.lod_basis: Crew briefing and safety awareness mechanism

supporting_text: On the evening of November 16,2020, the night drill crew came on tour and attended a pre tour safety meeting.

controls.name: Handover communication between day and night crews
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to communicate valve status and operational conditions between shifts
controls.lod_basis: Shift handover and information transfer mechanism

supporting_text: The day tour Derrickhand informed his relief, the night Derrickhand, that the valves were left in the open position after the cleaning was complete.

controls.name: Visual inspection of valve line-up
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify incorrect valve configuration before discharge occurs

supporting_text: The Derrickman decided to walk around and inspect the valve line-up discovering that the dump line valve was in the open position, allowing 458 barrels of synthetic based mud to dump into the Gulf of Mexico. The Derrickman immediately closed the valve and contacted the supervisors.

controls.name: Monitoring of pit fluid gains during transfer
controls.barrier_role: Detection control to identify abnormal fluid loss during transfer operations

supporting_text: As the transfer proceeded, the night crew noticed the Reserve pit and Return pit quit showing gains of fluid. Contact was made with the offshore supply vessel and requested that a second pump be switched on, thinking that the vessel was having problems with its first pump.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
